revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Manually new created pages after wiki restoration]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24593703458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Date Shrines]][[ja:伊達神社 (亀岡市)]] {{List of coordinates}} '''Date Shrine''' (だてじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] listed in the [[Engishiki]] [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Register of Deities]] under [[Tanba Province]] [[Kuwata District]], and is classified as an [[shikinaisha]]. The following two shrines are regarded as [[ronsha]] (candidate shrines): * 1. [[Date Shrine (Kameoka City)#Date Shrine (Utsune-chō, Kameoka City)|Date Shrine]] (1-2 Higashiura, Utsune-chō, [[Kameoka City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * 2. [[Date Shrine (Kameoka City)#Date Shrine (Amarube-chō, Kameoka City)|Date Shrine]] (16 Katsuka, Amarube-chō, Kameoka City, Kyoto Prefecture) == Historical Examination == There are two candidate shrines within Kameoka City, but both are currently small shrines with unclear origins. The relationship between the two is also uncertain, though one theory holds that the shrine in Utsune-chō was divided (bunshi) from the shrine in Amarube-chō,[https://web.archive.org/web/20160304114354/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380112-01.html Date Shrine] (Amarube-chō) (Survey of Engishiki Shrines [personal website]). and their annual festivals are held on the same day. Like other Date Shrines, the enshrined deity is [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]]. == Date Shrine (Utsune-chō, Kameoka City) == {{Shrine |Name = Date Shrine |Image = [[File:Date-jinja (Utsunecho, Kameoka) torii.JPG|280px]]
Torii |Location = 1-2 Higashiura, Utsune-chō, [[Kameoka City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |Coordinates= {{Wikicoord2|35|01|21.17|N|135|34|15.20|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Date Shrine (Utsune-chō, Kameoka City)}} |Deity = [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] |Sacred Object = |Status = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor) [[Ronsha]]
Former [[Village Shrine]] |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = One-bay [[Nagare-zukuri]] |Other Name = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = [[July 23]][https://web.archive.org/web/20160304111533/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/tanba/bun/tab380112-02.html Date Shrine] (Utsune-chō) (Survey of Engishiki Shrines [personal website]). |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Date Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|01|21.17|N|135|34|15.20|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|01|21.17|N|135|34|15.20|E}}}} }} '''Date Shrine''' (だてじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Utsune-chō, [[Kameoka City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a [[shikinaisha]] and a [[ronsha]]. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a [[Village Shrine]]. === Enshrined Deity === * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] === History === In ancient times, it was a subsidiary shrine within the grounds of the neighboring Hōzō-ji Temple,The precinct explanation board states “Hōzō-ji” as 宝蔵寺, which may be a mistake. but in [[Enkyō]] 4 (1747), flooding of the [[Ōi River]] washed it away along with Hōzō-ji, resulting in the loss of records.From the precinct explanation board. One theory holds that a villa of [[Fujiwara no Hidehira]] of the [[Northern Fujiwara clan]], specifically that of his third son [[Fujiwara no Tadahira|Tadahira]], was located in Utsune, and that this shrine was established to enshrine his spirit, with the villa later becoming Hōzō-ji Temple. However, since the establishment of shikinaisha predates this period, it is believed that the shrine was used to enshrine Tadahira’s spirit at a later time.“Date Shrine” entry in *Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture*. During the [[Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] in the Meiji period, the shrine was relocated to its present site and became independent. === Precincts === The main hall, a one-bay [[Nagare-zukuri]] structure, is enshrined within a protective building. An inscription on the foundation indicates it was constructed in [[Kansei]] 1 (1789). File:Date-jinja (Utsunecho, Kameoka) honden.JPG|Protective building of the main hall === Auxiliary Shrines === * Inari Shrine == Date Shrine (Amarube-chō, Kameoka City) == {{Shrine |Name = Date Shrine |Image = [[File:Date-jinja (Amarubecho, Kameoka) torii.JPG|250px]]
Torii and protective building of the main hall |Location = 16 Katsuka, Amarube-chō, [[Kameoka City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |Coordinates= {{Wikicoord2|35|00|53.30|N|135|34|13.07|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|name=Date Shrine (Amarube-chō, Kameoka City)}} |Deity = [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] |Sacred Object = |Status = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor) [[Ronsha]]
Former [[Unranked Shrine]] |Founded = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other Name = |Pilgrimage = |Festival = [[July 23]] |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Date Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|00|53.30|N|135|34|13.07|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|00|53.30|N|135|34|13.07|E}}}} }} '''Date Shrine''' (だてじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Amarube-chō, [[Kameoka City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a [[shikinaisha]] and a [[ronsha]]. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine ranking system]], it was classified as an [[Unranked Shrine]] (this does not mean it had no status; “Unranked Shrine” was itself a category). === Enshrined Deity === * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto]] === History === Its origins are unknown. It is said that the Date Shrine in Utsune-chō was divided from this shrine. === Precincts === The precincts are very small, containing two shrine buildings. The stone torii was constructed in [[Genna]] 5 (1619). File:Date-jinja (Amarubecho, Kameoka) keidai.JPG|Protective building of the main hall (front) and precinct Inari Shrine (rear) === Auxiliary Shrines === * Inari Shrine == Notes == {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} == References == {{Commons|Category:Date-jinja (Utsunecho, Kameoka)|Date Shrine (Utsune-chō, Kameoka City)}} {{Commons|Category:Date-jinja (Amarubecho, Kameoka)|Date Shrine (Amarube-chō, Kameoka City)}} * Precinct explanation boards * *Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture* (Heibonsha), Kameoka City, “Date Shrine” entry {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Dateshrine Kameoka}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kameoka City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka City]] {{wikidata link|Q135040498}}
